# The sesam-secret merge driver decrypts the three sides of a secret, does a
# 3-way text merge on the plaintext and - when both branches touched the same
# region - writes conflict markers into the revealed file so the user can
# resolve them and re-seal.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Stash the three content versions outside the worktree: sesam's hooks run an
# aggressive clean that wipes untracked files under the sesam dir (the worktree
# root here), which would otherwise delete these helpers before we use them.
cp base.txt $TMPDIR/base.txt
cp ours.txt $TMPDIR/ours.txt
cp theirs.txt $TMPDIR/theirs.txt

# Init sesam and install the merge driver git-config (the .gitattributes
# mapping secrets to merge=sesam-merge-secret is always written by init).
exec sesam init --user admin --install-merge
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Base version XB of the secret, sealed and committed on the default branch.
cp $TMPDIR/base.txt secret.txt
exec sesam add secret.txt --group admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'add secret (XB)'

# Branch OURS: XB -> XO.
exec git checkout -b ours
cp $TMPDIR/ours.txt secret.txt
exec sesam seal
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'ours: XO'

# Branch THEIRS branches off the XB commit (ours~1) and changes it to XT.
exec git checkout -b theirs ours~1
cp $TMPDIR/theirs.txt secret.txt
exec sesam seal
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'theirs: XT'

# Merge THEIRS into OURS. Both sides changed the same line, so the driver must
# report a conflict: git merge exits non-zero.
exec git checkout ours
! exec git merge theirs

# The revealed plaintext carries standard git conflict markers with both sides.
grep '^<<<<<<< ' secret.txt
grep '^=======$' secret.txt
grep '^>>>>>>> ' secret.txt
grep 'secret = O' secret.txt
grep 'secret = T' secret.txt

-- base.txt --
line one
secret = B
line three

-- ours.txt --
line one
secret = O
line three

-- theirs.txt --
line one
secret = T
line three
